proposed_conditioned_material_variables.specimen_geometry_and_exposure: Details regarding the physical shape, dimension, exposed surface area, and masking techniques applied to the specimen to restrict corrosion to flat surfaces and prevent interference from edge, end-grain, or crevice attack.

proposed_experiment_variables.electrolyte_chemistry_and_pH: The specific chemical constituents, concentrations (e.g., 3.5% NaCl, 10% FeCl3), exact pH level, and dissolved gas status (e.g., nitrogenated/deaerated) of the test solution.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_pitting_temperature: The precise threshold temperature (often designated as CPT or T500) at which stable localized pitting initiates under a constant applied potential or specific freely corroding environmental condition.
proposed_experiment_variables.pitting_initiation_criterion: The quantitative threshold (such as a specific current density rise to 10 µA/cm²) or qualitative metric (such as visual confirmation after a specific time interval) used by the researchers to definitively mark the onset of stable pitting.
